message:
fix(doctor): always verify page-warning absence after warning repair

`warning_repair_verified` previously only required `report_has_warn_level_page_anomaly`
to be clear when the *current* doctor run had attempted a page-anomaly
repair. That left a hole: if a page-level integrity warning was
surfaced by a *different* repair (e.g. blocked-cache-stale rebuild
exposed a "Page N: never used" finding from `sqlite3.integrity_check`),
the verification gate would still report success.

Drop the `repaired_page_anomalies` parameter and unconditionally
require the page-anomaly finding to be absent in the post-repair
report. Page-level warnings cannot be ignored just because we did not
intend to fix them on this run — if integrity_check is warning, the
db is not "verified clean".

Two existing tests updated for the new signature, plus a regression
test (`warning_repair_verified_rejects_page_warning_introduced_by_other_repair`)
that pins the new behavior: a page warning with `repaired_page_anomalies=false`
(no longer a parameter; modeled by "we did not try to repair pages this
run") must still cause verification to fail when the warning is present.
